Description: Every input needs a visible label. Placeholder text is not a label.
Error messages should be helpful and non-blaming. Show them near the field, not just at the top.
Do: Show helpful error messages near field

Description: Clearly indicate required fields. Don't rely on asterisks alone—some users don't understand them.
All interactive elements need visible focus indicators. Don't remove outline without replacement.
Tab order should follow visual order and be logical.
Code Example Good: Logical DOM order matches visual order

Description: Show feedback during async operations. Use skeleton screens or spinners.
Update UI immediately, sync later. Makes interactions feel fast.
Start simple, reveal sophistication through interaction. Basic options first, advanced behind expandable sections.
Design empty states that teach the interface, not just say 'nothing here'.
Not every button should be primary. Use ghost buttons, text links, secondary styles. Hierarchy matters.
